prevalence.trend: stable; seasonal increases with viral outbreaks

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Acute cough with sputum after URI; rule out pneumonia (fever, focal chest findings) and asthma exacerbation

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with supportive care; antibiotics usually not indicated

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis in primary care; chest X-ray for suspected pneumonia
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Supportive care, bronchodilators if wheezing; avoid routine antibiotics
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Antibiotic overuse and difficulty distinguishing from pneumonia in resource-limited settings
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Primary care access and clinical pathways for ARIs
